title: 28 Years Later: The Bone Temple - Roger Ebert Review
text: Roger Ebert's review site covers 28 Years Later: The Bone Temple as part of its ongoing film criticism. The film represents a significant entry in the long-running 28 Years Later franchise, with this sequel continuing immediately after the previous installment's events. The film is directed by Nia DaCosta, who brings her distinctive visual style to the horror sequel. Ralph Fiennes' performance as Dr. Ian Kelson is highlighted as a key strength, with the actor delivering a nuanced portrayal of the survivalist medic. Jack O'Connell's performance as the charismatic and dangerous Sir Lord Jimmy Crystal provides the film's antagonistic force. The film's approach to the zombie/infected subgenre is described as innovative, moving beyond traditional zombie horror into territory that explores themes of faith, compassion, and survival. The combination of strong performances, direction, and thematic depth makes the film a notable entry in horror cinema, despite its commercial underperformance.
